  "text": "The Raft consensus integration and range lease management in pkg/kv/kvserver/ spans several key files:\n\n## Raft Consensus Integration\n\n**replica_raft.go** — The primary file for Raft processing on a Replica. Contains `Replica.handleRaftReady` (the entry point, acquires raftMu then delegates) and `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` (the core method that interfaces with etcd/raft's RawNode, processes Ready structs, appends to log, sends messages, and triggers state machine application). Also contains `handleRaftReadyStats` struct.\n\n**store_raft.go** — Contains `Store.processReady(rangeID)`, which is called by the Raft scheduler goroutine to drive Raft processing for a given range. It looks up the replica and calls `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`. Also contains `Store.HandleSnapshot` and related plumbing.\n\n**scheduler.go** — The `raftScheduler` and `raftProcessor` interface (with `processReady` method). The scheduler coordinates which ranges need Raft processing and dispatches to worker goroutines.\n\n**replica_application_state_machine.go** — Implements the `replicaStateMachine` which applies committed Raft log entries to the replica's state. Called from within `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`.\n\n**replica_app_batch.go** / **app_batch.go** — Implements batch application of Raft commands to RocksDB/Pebble.\n\n**replica_application_cmd.go**, **replica_application_decoder.go**, **replica_application_result.go** — Supporting types for the command application pipeline.\n\n**apply/task.go**, **apply/cmd.go** — Generic Raft log application framework (Task, Command interfaces).\n\n**replica_proposal.go**, **replica_proposal_buf.go** — Proposal creation and buffering; the proposal buffer flushes proposals into Raft via `FlushLockedWithRaftGroup`, called during `handleRaftReady`.\n\n**replica_raftstorage.go** — Implements `raft.Storage` interface for the replica (log access, snapshot creation).\n\n**replica_raft_quiesce.go** — Quiescence logic for idle Raft groups.\n\n**raft.go** — Raft-related helpers and types used across the store.\n\n**logstore/** — Raft log persistence layer.\n\n## Range Lease Management\n\n**replica_range_lease.go** — The central file for lease management. Contains `pendingLeaseRequest` (tracks in-flight lease requests), `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` (sends the actual lease RPC), `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` (initiates a lease request with quota limiting), `Replica.AdminTransferLease` (transfers lease to another store), and `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` (decides when to request a new lease).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_request.go** — Implements the `RequestLease` command that is evaluated below Raft to acquire a new lease (both expiration and epoch types).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_transfer.go** — Implements the `TransferLease` command evaluated below Raft to transfer the lease to another replica.\n\n**leases/build.go** — Logic to build the next lease, including `leaseType()` (chooses expiration vs epoch), `leaseExpiration()` (sets expiration time for expiration leases), and `leaseEpoch()` (sets epoch for epoch-based leases).\n\n**leases/status.go** — `LeaseStatus` computation — determines if a lease is valid, expired, or needs renewal.\n\n**leases/verify.go** — Lease verification logic.\n\n**leases/errors.go** — Lease-related error types.\n\n## Liveness-Based Node Health\n\n**liveness/liveness.go** — Defines `NodeLiveness` (the main liveness tracker), `NodeLivenessOptions`, `GetIsLiveMap()`, and the heartbeat loop. Node liveness records feed directly into epoch-based lease decisions.\n\n**liveness/cache.go** — `Cache` with `getIsLiveMap()` and `getLiveness()` — caches liveness records for fast access during lease status evaluation.\n\n**liveness/storage.go** — KV storage layer for liveness records.\n\n**liveness/livenesspb/liveness.go** — `NodeVitalityInterface` (the interface that lease code uses for liveness queries), `LivenessStatus()` function, and `IsLiveMap` type.\n\n**replica_store_liveness.go** — Integration between the replica and the store liveness subsystem (storeliveness package).\n\nThe call chain for Raft-driven processing is: `raftScheduler` → `Store.processReady` → `Replica.handleRaftReady` → `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` → `replicaStateMachine.ApplyCommittedEntries`. For lease acquisition: `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` → `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` → `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` → `RequestLease` (batcheval) or `TransferLease` (batcheval). Epoch-based leases consult `NodeLiveness` to check and increment node epochs; expiration-based leases use wall-clock timestamps."
}

  "text": "The Raft consensus integration and range lease management in pkg/kv/kvserver/ spans several key files:\n\n## Raft Consensus Integration\n\n**replica_raft.go** — The primary file for Raft processing on a Replica. Contains `Replica.handleRaftReady` (the entry point, acquires raftMu then delegates) and `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` (the core method that interfaces with etcd/raft's RawNode, processes Ready structs, appends to log, sends messages, and triggers state machine application). Also defines `handleRaftReadyStats`.\n\n**store_raft.go** — Contains `Store.processReady(rangeID)`, which is called by the Raft scheduler goroutine to drive Raft processing for a given range. It looks up the replica and calls `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`. Also handles `Store.HandleSnapshot`.\n\n**scheduler.go** — The `raftScheduler` and `raftProcessor` interface (with `processReady` method). The scheduler coordinates which ranges need Raft processing and dispatches to worker goroutines.\n\n**replica_application_state_machine.go** — Implements the `replicaStateMachine` which applies committed Raft log entries to the replica's state. Called from within `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`.\n\n**replica_app_batch.go** / **app_batch.go** — Implements batch application of Raft commands to Pebble.\n\n**replica_application_cmd.go**, **replica_application_decoder.go**, **replica_application_result.go** — Supporting types for the command application pipeline.\n\n**apply/task.go**, **apply/cmd.go** — Generic Raft log application framework (Task, Command interfaces).\n\n**replica_proposal.go**, **replica_proposal_buf.go** — Proposal creation and buffering; the proposal buffer flushes proposals into Raft via `FlushLockedWithRaftGroup`, called during `handleRaftReady`.\n\n**replica_raftstorage.go** — Implements `raft.Storage` interface for the replica (log access, snapshot creation).\n\n**replica_raft_quiesce.go** — Quiescence logic for idle Raft groups.\n\n**raft.go** — Raft-related helpers and types used across the store.\n\n## Range Lease Management\n\n**replica_range_lease.go** — The central file for lease management. Contains `pendingLeaseRequest` (tracks in-flight lease requests), `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` (sends the actual lease RPC), `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` (initiates a lease request with quota limiting), `Replica.AdminTransferLease` (transfers lease to another store), and `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` (decides when to request a new lease).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_request.go** — Implements the `RequestLease` command evaluated below Raft to acquire a new lease (both expiration and epoch types).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_transfer.go** — Implements the `TransferLease` command evaluated below Raft to transfer the lease to another replica.\n\n**leases/build.go** — Logic to build the next lease, including `leaseType()` (chooses expiration vs epoch), `leaseExpiration()` (sets expiration time for expiration leases), and `leaseEpoch()` (sets epoch for epoch-based leases). Expiration leases use wall-clock timestamps while epoch leases rely on the node liveness epoch.\n\n**leases/status.go** — `LeaseStatus` computation: determines if a lease is valid, expired, or needs renewal.\n\n**leases/verify.go** — Lease verification logic.\n\n**leases/errors.go** — Lease-related error types.\n\n## Liveness-Based Node Health\n\n**liveness/liveness.go** — Defines `NodeLiveness` (the main liveness tracker), `NodeLivenessOptions`, `GetIsLiveMap()`, and the heartbeat loop. Node liveness records feed directly into epoch-based lease decisions (a node's liveness epoch must be current for its epoch-based lease to be valid).\n\n**liveness/cache.go** — `Cache` with `getIsLiveMap()` and `getLiveness()` — caches liveness records for fast access during lease status evaluation.\n\n**liveness/storage.go** — KV storage layer for liveness records.\n\n**liveness/livenesspb/liveness.go** — `NodeVitalityInterface` (the interface lease code uses for liveness queries), `LivenessStatus()` function, and `IsLiveMap` type.\n\n**replica_store_liveness.go** — Integration between the replica and the store liveness subsystem.\n\n## Call Chain\n\nRaft-driven processing: `raftScheduler` (scheduler.go) → `Store.processReady` (store_raft.go) → `Replica.handleRaftReady` (replica_raft.go) → `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` (replica_raft.go) → `replicaStateMachine.ApplyCommittedEntries` (replica_application_state_machine.go).\n\nLease acquisition: `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` → `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` → `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` (replica_range_lease.go) → `RequestLease` batcheval (cmd_lease_request.go). Epoch leases consult `NodeLiveness` to check/increment node epochs; expiration leases use wall-clock timestamps computed in `leases/build.go`."
}

  "text": "The Raft consensus integration and range lease management in pkg/kv/kvserver/ spans several key files:\n\n## Raft Consensus Integration\n\n**replica_raft.go** — The primary file for Raft processing on a Replica. Contains `Replica.handleRaftReady` (the entry point, acquires raftMu then delegates) and `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` (the core method that interfaces with etcd/raft's RawNode, processes Ready structs, appends to log, sends messages, and triggers state machine application). Also defines `handleRaftReadyStats`.\n\n**store_raft.go** — Contains `Store.processReady(rangeID)`, which is called by the Raft scheduler goroutine to drive Raft processing for a given range. It looks up the replica and calls `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`. Also handles `Store.HandleSnapshot`.\n\n**scheduler.go** — The `raftScheduler` and `raftProcessor` interface (with `processReady` method). The scheduler coordinates which ranges need Raft processing and dispatches to worker goroutines.\n\n**replica_application_state_machine.go** — Implements the `replicaStateMachine` which applies committed Raft log entries to the replica's state. Called from within `handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked`.\n\n**replica_app_batch.go** / **app_batch.go** — Implements batch application of Raft commands to Pebble.\n\n**replica_application_cmd.go**, **replica_application_decoder.go**, **replica_application_result.go** — Supporting types for the command application pipeline.\n\n**apply/task.go**, **apply/cmd.go** — Generic Raft log application framework (Task, Command interfaces).\n\n**replica_proposal.go**, **replica_proposal_buf.go** — Proposal creation and buffering; the proposal buffer flushes proposals into Raft via `FlushLockedWithRaftGroup`, called during `handleRaftReady`.\n\n**replica_raftstorage.go** — Implements `raft.Storage` interface for the replica (log access, snapshot creation).\n\n**replica_raft_quiesce.go** — Quiescence logic for idle Raft groups.\n\n**raft.go** — Raft-related helpers and types used across the store.\n\n## Range Lease Management\n\n**replica_range_lease.go** — The central file for lease management. Contains `pendingLeaseRequest` (tracks in-flight lease requests), `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` (sends the actual lease RPC), `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` (initiates a lease request with quota limiting), `Replica.AdminTransferLease` (transfers lease to another store), and `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` (decides when to request a new lease).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_request.go** — Implements the `RequestLease` command evaluated below Raft to acquire a new lease (both expiration and epoch types).\n\n**batcheval/cmd_lease_transfer.go** — Implements the `TransferLease` command evaluated below Raft to transfer the lease to another replica.\n\n**leases/build.go** — Logic to build the next lease, including `leaseType()` (chooses expiration vs epoch), `leaseExpiration()` (sets expiration time for expiration leases), and `leaseEpoch()` (sets epoch for epoch-based leases). Expiration leases use wall-clock timestamps while epoch leases rely on the node liveness epoch.\n\n**leases/status.go** — `LeaseStatus` computation: determines if a lease is valid, expired, or needs renewal.\n\n**leases/verify.go** — Lease verification logic.\n\n**leases/errors.go** — Lease-related error types.\n\n## Liveness-Based Node Health\n\n**liveness/liveness.go** — Defines `NodeLiveness` (the main liveness tracker), `NodeLivenessOptions`, `GetIsLiveMap()`, and the heartbeat loop. Node liveness records feed directly into epoch-based lease decisions (a node's liveness epoch must be current for its epoch-based lease to be valid).\n\n**liveness/cache.go** — `Cache` with `getIsLiveMap()` and `getLiveness()` — caches liveness records for fast access during lease status evaluation.\n\n**liveness/storage.go** — KV storage layer for liveness records.\n\n**liveness/livenesspb/liveness.go** — `NodeVitalityInterface` (the interface lease code uses for liveness queries), `LivenessStatus()` function, and `IsLiveMap` type.\n\n**replica_store_liveness.go** — Integration between the replica and the store liveness subsystem.\n\n## Call Chain\n\nRaft-driven processing: `raftScheduler` (scheduler.go) → `Store.processReady` (store_raft.go) → `Replica.handleRaftReady` (replica_raft.go) → `Replica.handleRaftReadyRaftMuLocked` (replica_raft.go) → `replicaStateMachine.ApplyCommittedEntries` (replica_application_state_machine.go).\n\nLease acquisition: `Replica.shouldRequestLeaseRLocked` → `Replica.requestLeaseLocked` → `pendingLeaseRequest.requestLease` (replica_range_lease.go) → `RequestLease` batcheval (cmd_lease_request.go). Epoch leases consult `NodeLiveness` to check/increment node epochs; expiration leases use wall-clock timestamps computed in `leases/build.go`."
